Therefore, it would be incorrect to state that any primary sensory ending conveys information via 1a fibers—the terminology belongs exclusively to efferent pathways. This distinction reinforces why option C is indeed false while others hold valid descriptions about structure/function relationships in sensory endings.

Therefore, these fibers cannot be described as conducting through "primary" sensory endings since they belong to an entirely different functional category.

Answer: C...
